notes: Anthropic develops Claude AI. Their AI Safety Levels (ASL) framework defines risk thresholds for AI capabilities as part of the Responsible Scaling Policy.

data.description: Risk threshold framework defining safety levels for AI capabilities, from baseline (ASL-1) to catastrophic risk (ASL-4).
data.notes: ASL levels define escalating risk thresholds and corresponding safety requirements. Part of Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy. ASL-1 through ASL-4 represent increasing capability and risk, each requiring progressively stricter containment and evaluation measures. No per-item URLs — levels are described in the Responsible Scaling Policy document.

versions_available.note: Current version of the Responsible Scaling Policy.

data.note: Conceptual safety levels — no individual item URLs. Refer to the Responsible Scaling Policy for full descriptions of each level's requirements and thresholds.

examples.note: Current level for frontier models — some uplift but below catastrophic threshold
Significantly elevated risk — requires enhanced containment
Catastrophic risk level — strictest controls required
